title: 1991 Rolex 16700 Pumpkin Pepsi GMT
description: Description: The 16700 is sandwiched between the 16750 reference and the 16710. The 3175 movement includes a quickset for the date but forgoes the jumping hour hands the 16710 is known for. This pumpkin colored dial is pure aces. Case has been recently polished and is very thick and sharp. Dial is very glossy and free of any cracks or spidering. Dial: Pumpkin GMT Insert: Pepsi Case: Excellent condition case Bracelet: Oyster

title: 1997 Rolex 16520 White Panda Dial Zenith Daytona
description: Description: The 16520 can be considered the 993 generation Porsche of the Daytona line up. It has a robust Zenith chronograph movement and was the first 40mm Daytona to be introduced to the Rolex line up. The white panda variant dial gives off a nice contrast between the subdials. The case is in excellent shape and the lume on the dial is a light cream which is consistent with the tritinova (tritium marked dial / luminova lume) run of watches in 1997. The case is excellent and has sharp thick l

title: 1967 Rolex 1016 “Day-Glow” Explorer I
description: Description : Early matte Frog Foot dials, serial encasement 1’5-3’5mil and 4’4-5mil, were lumed with zinc sulfide and tritium whose potent combination allowed still for them to glow even in the sun. This 1016 is a unique Day-Glow example and surely wont last too long on the site. Case : 36mm Stainless Steel, Excellent Condition Dial : Frog Foot, Zinc Sulfide ‘Day Glow’ Bracelet : Year Correct Oyster Rivet

title: 1980 Rolex 16808 Yellow Gold Faded Insert Submariner
description: Description: The 16808 is a stunner when seen on a blue nipple dial. These were only produced for a few years as Rolex eventually removed the nipple dials to match the gold surrounds of their steel sport dials. The nipple dials were at this point only unique to Gold and Two-Tone watches marking them as special pieces. This 16808 with its unique blue dial and faded insert has all the subtle tones of vintage while providing the heft of a neo-vintage piece. Case is excellent and thick. Bracelet is

title: 1978 Rolex 1675/8 Yellow Gold Tropical GMT
description: Description: It’s hard to describe a watch as unique as this. These Gold rootbeer GMT’s standout from a crowd of gold watches. The oxblood Soleil dial has a bengal stripe pattern developing, unearthing the brassy lighter colored dial underneath. The tritium / gold nipple markers date this watch to a standalone period for Rolex during their nipple dial run. This is a unique dial and some will hate it and others will gravitate to a one of a kind dial. As we look closer we see that the case is unpo

title: 1969 Rolex 1601 Grey Dial Datejust
description: Description: An excellent 36 mm white gold surround date just. Dial is a slate grey. It has beautiful deep yellow loop plots that are full. Case is excellent as well and appears long and thick. Dial: Slate grey dial Insert: Black Insert Case: Excellent Case Bracelet: Rolex Jubilee Bracelet

title: 2023 Rolex 116509 Blue Dial White Gold Daytona W/Box & Paper
description: Description: Excellent full set 116509 white gold Daytona with blue dial. Watch comes as a complete set and is an excellent condition appears unpolished. Dial: Blue Insert: White Gold Bezel Case: Excellent Case Bracelet: Rolex Oyster Bracelet

title: 1979 Rolex 16750 Ghost GMT
description: Description: When we get asked what Rolex are the best investments without having to give up an arm and a leg, we point them to neo-vintage pieces. The 16750 GMT sits on the early side of the neo-vintage scale coming shortly after the 1675 classic plexiglass variant. This 16750 has a beautiful matte dial and deep yellow lume paired with a faded black insert that appears like a blue slate color. The case is in excellent shape and appears lightly polished. title: Grand Seiko Spring Drive SBGE275 GMT – Box and Papers
description: Description : The white dial variant of the SBGE275 with GMT function Case : 44mm Steel Dial : White Bezel : GMT Bracelet : Steel

title: 1968 Rolex 1500 Tropical Oyster Perpetual -Date-
description: Description : All of us at GP sport a 34mm daily watch and there are few better than the vintage Oyster Perpetual lineup. Don’t let the size fool you, its 34mm case is largely made up of the dial and really indistinguishable from its bigger 36mm brother the Datejust. This specific 1500 sports a tropical dial that once was blue. Over the years, UV, humidity, and heat has caused this dial to morph into something special. The white text is still all intact leaving us to believe this was not water-d

title: 1971 Rolex 1601 Tropical Datejust
description: Description: An excellent first or last vintage Rolex addition. This 1601 has a beautiful wabi-sabi tropical dial that has turned from a black glossy dial. The crystal has been upgraded to a true dome crystal and omits the cluttered cyclops on the crystal. Lume plots are all present and have a vibrant yellow colored lume. Case is excellent as well. Dial: Tropical Dial Insert: Fluted Case: Excellent thick case Bracelet: Rolex Jubilee

title: 1991 Rolex 16570 Chi-Chi Polar Explorer II
description: Description: “ChicChi” Polar Explorer II refers to the extreme yellow coloring present on the hands and dials of this Explorer II. These are one of the most desirable Explorer II’s and have traded at huge premiums. The case is excellent and is thick and even throughout the case. These saturated lume Explorer II’s are getting increasingly hard to find in this color and condition. We typically see this deeper coloring in 1991 range watches and this one lines up perfectly. Dial: Chi Chi Polar dial

title: 1960 Rolex 6075 yellow Gold Overtone Oyster Perpetual
description: Description: There Ovettone Rolex watches are known for their chunkier size despite their 34mm case. Some were made in 14k Gold while others like this one were made in 18k. This one has a 14k Rolex buckle which would be kosher as well. This dial has no lume and carries with it a roulette date wheel. The patina on the dial is like a fine grain and adds to its character. Dial: Ivory patina Case: Excellent Condition Bracelet: Leather Strap with 14k Rolex Tang and Buckle

title: Patek Philippe 5054R Rose Gold Moonphase
description: Description: An excellent yellow gold watch only 5054R in Rose Gold. Insert: n/a Dial: White Case: 36mm White Roman Dial Bracelet: Leather

title: Patek Philippe 5159R-001 Perpetual Calendar W/Box & Paper With Winder
description: Description: A classic from the great house of Patek, the 5159R finished in a rose gold case seems like a watched dug up from the archives, however this reference was only introduced in 2007. The case sits at a jumbo size of 38mm making it feel more contemporary than its vintage counterparts. This reference draws from military trench watches which were large modified pocket watches with added lugs to make it wearable on the wrist. This piece comes complete with a full set and winder. Insert: n/a

title: 1979 Rolex 1680 Submariner
description: Description: Date function, larger lume plots, and valued far less than its no-date iterations make Mk.1 1680s the perfect matte dial Submariner.This one has an excellent light lume tritium dial and correct insert and killer case. Insert: Black Mk3 Dial: Matte Mk1 Case: 40mm Stainless Steel, Appears Unpolished Bracelet: Stainless Steel Oyster

description: Description: The 1601 Datejust is simply our favorite everyday watch. It pairs well with anything you throw at it whether it’s denim or a cashmere single button suit. This dial has beautiful full lume plots and is in excellent shape. We are partial to the 4 digit Datejusts especially the 1601’s due to their white gold fluted surrounds and exclusive pie pan dial that gives the watch depth along with the raised plexiglass crystal. This black glossy gilt dial is free from scratches or any eyesores

title: 1966 Rolex 1803 Yellow Gold Black Dial Day-Date
description: Description: Matte Black Dial 1803s are our favorite. This sleeve-worn case remains thick and even. Hands are non-lume gold hands. Dial retains all-original lume plots. Case: 18k Yellow Gold, Sleeve Polished Dial: Matte Black, Gold Applliqué Bracelet: Leather Strap with Rolex buckle

title: 1979 Rolex 6263 “Big Red” Silver Daytona
description: Description: There is something about the Big Red 6263. Considered the blue print for all modern Daytona’s with its signature Big Red Daytona text , to its black bezel and chunky screw down pushers, the classic 36mm variant was an instant classic in our eyes. Rolex made many variants of the 36mm Daytona from the pre-daytona to the Newman Exotic dials to this specific Big Red but this variant is always the version we think of when the word Daytona comes to mind. This specific silver 6263 is in ex

title: 1973 Rolex 1680 Tropical Purple Yellow Gold Submariner
description: Description: The early nipple dial era of Submariner’s in yellow gold and blue dial variants sometimes are known to change color. Well this is a full color shift from factory blue into techno purple. This watch sold before it hit the site, and for good reason. Watch only, and what a watch it is. Dial: Nipple Tropical Purple Insert: Blue Case: 40mm Yellow Gold Bracelet: Leather Strap

title: 1964 Rolex 6240 Black Dial “Floating Daytona”
description: Description: The 6240 can be said to have birthed the screw down pusher Daytona lineup. Introduced in the early 60s this is one of the least understood 4 digit Daytona references and one of the fewest ones produced with production numbers hovering around 2000. These early models are unique with individual characteristics that stick out to the seasoned Daytona collectors. The MK1 bezel is married to the earliest pushers, the MK0, and the first appearance of the nickel plated brass pushers found o

title: 1997 Rolex 16528 Yellow Gold Black Diamond Dial Zenith Daytona w/ Box
description: Description: The 16528 is the Zenith era Gold Daytona model. These utilized movements from Zenith and many argue this to be the best iteration of the modern Daytona case and dial. The testament to this is seeing Rolex move back to the dial style of the 1652X series in their latest update of the Ceramic Daytona series. This model has a rare black diamond indices model which is not often seen and often overlooked. We feel these pieces are truly special considering their pave siblings are commandin

title: 1991 Rolex 16710 Pumpkin Coke GMT
description: Description: Coke variegations, originally introduced on the 16760, but later evolved into the 16710 reference who appears also in Pepsi and all-Black. This GMT-Master II comes from 1991 which is known to produce pumpkin dials in much higher frequency. Hands are lighter which is normal on these pieces. Case: 40mm Stainless Steel Insert: “Coke” Black and Red Dial: Gloss Black, Pumpkin Tritium Bracelet: 78360

title: Omega 3570.50.00 Galaxy Express 999 Speedmaster w Mitsukoshi Dial Mod
description: Description: Omega has always had a soft spot for the Japanese market and this Speedmaster is a example of that. The 3570.50 “Galaxy Express 999” is a limited edition of 1999 pieces based off the manga, exclusively to the Japanese market. On the wrist it wears just like any other speedmaster, but hidden on the case back is the engraving of the character “Maetel“ and the signature of “Leiji Matsumoto” the creator of the manga. title: 1972 Rolex 6694 Tropical Oysterdate
description: Description : Manual wind and 34mm doesnt get better than these OPs. Faded tropical dial , excellent unpolished case. Dial: Tropical Insert: Smooth Case: Excellent condition case Bracelet: Folded Oyster

title: 1970 Rolex 1665 Double Red Tropical Sea-Dweller
description: Description: When we talk about Double Red Sea-Dwellers, the MK2 , thin case variant, can be considered the grail amongst all other dwellers. These MK2’s are married to a thinner case than the other SD variants. MK2 1665’s are also more prone to turn tropical as is in this case as well. We don’t know if anyone needs a Tropical DRSD but the want is very very strong. The insert has also begun fading to a greyish slate color which contrasts beautifully with the explosion of browns blacks yellow’s a

title: 1987 Rolex 15037 Yellow Gold Oyster Perpetual – Date –
description: Description: At 34mm this 15037 Gold Oyster Perpetual is just concentrated goodness. The heft and density of the gold in a smaller package makes this feel very luxurious especially when compared to their 36mm bigger siblings. The blue dial contrasts nicely and compliments well to the 18k yellow gold case. Dial: Blue Insert: Gold Case: Excellent condition case Bracelet : Leather

title: 1965 Omega 105.003-65 ST “Ed-White” Tropical Speedmaster
description: Description: 1965 Speedmaster 105.003-65. When NASA qualified the Speedmaster for manned space flight it was the 105.003 making this perhaps the most iconic reference in the watches history. This watch has all the things one looks for in a vintage speedy. Despite the nickname this dial is anything but white. title: 1960 Rolex 5512 Tropical Gilt Tulip Submariner W/Box & Paper
description: Description: Possibly the finest tropical Submariner we’ve ever handled, this 5512 Tropical Gilt Chapter Ring stands out with an extraordinary caramel-toned dial and a case that appears untouched and unpolished. We may never encounter another example quite this special, making it all the more memorable to have passed through our hands. Original Box & Paper with the watch Dial: Caramel-gold tropical gilt Insert: Brownish-grey “Long 5” insert Case: Excellent condition, appears unpolished Bracelet:

title: 1998 Rolex 18296 Platinum Diamond Set Day-Date
description: Description: Ever since Rolex launched the Day-Date in 1956, it has been the brand’s most popular and prestigious lineages. The “President” nickname was earned after being worn by United States president Lyndon B. Johnson. This 18296 Day-Date takes the regular Day-Date most commonly made from Gold and amps it up by using platinum with a silver diamond dial. But if that wasn’t enough, the conservative watchmakers at Rolex also embedded diamonds into the lugs from the factory to give it more pop.

title: 1979 Benrus Type I
description: Description: The Department of defenses contracted Berns to build these watches between 1972-1980. 3 Types were issued with the type 1 being the rarest with nothing other than indices on the marker. Welded spring bars make this a nato only watch Insert: Rotating Dial: Markers Case: Excellent condition case Bracelet: Nato

title: 1962 Rolex 6098 Oyster Perpetual

Dial: White Tropical Dial Bezel: Smooth Hands: Original Tritium Hands Case: 36mm Stainless Steel, Excellent condition Bracelet: Brown Leather

The chronograph movement is powered by Valjouxs 72, the same caliber used in Rolexs 4-digit Daytonas of the same era. Steel case, aluminum bezel, and Panda dial show excellent. title: 1987 Rolex 16018 Yellow Gold Blue Khanjar Dial Datejust
description: Description: Yellow Gold Datejusts with Blue Sunburst dials are rare Yellow Gold Datejusts with Blue Sunburst Khanjar stamped dials are almost never seen. Omani Khanjar daggers represent the virtue of status; symbol of the Sultanate, of manhood, used only to be bloodied, seeking of revenge or brandished in self-defense. title: 1940’s Bovet Mono-Rattrapante Chronograph
description: Description: Having roots originating in pocketwatches, Bovet introduced this oversized 1070 Mono-rattrapante chronograph. Watchpool24 recently described its movement as; There was a strong demand for a split-seconds complication in the 1930s. As a result, Bovet developed and patented the Mono-Rattrapante mechanism in 1936. The chronograph starts by pressing the top pusher. Pressing and holding the bottom pusher will stop the second hand.
